message:
test(fuzz): add canonicalize_map permutation/duplicate/cross-type fuzz target (resolves r5v32)

canonicalize_map (lib.rs:515-581) implements RFC 8949 §4.2.3:
encoded-key lex sort with byte-equal duplicate rejection. The existing
canonicalize_map_deterministic target asserts same-Value determinism,
encode→decode→encode round-trip, and the size cap — but NOT the
metamorphic relations a sort regression would silently break.

Three MRs:
- permutation invariance: same pairs in any input order canonicalize
  identical (regression: sorting by insertion order would still pass
  determinism + round-trip but break this)
- duplicate-key rejection: appending an exact-duplicate key MUST trip
  DuplicateMapKey (Value::Map is a Vec, not a deduping HashMap)
- cross-type lex ordering anchor: a single map mixing int 0, bytes b'',
  text '', bool false, null — keys MUST emerge sorted by their CBOR
  head bytes 0x00 < 0x40 < 0x60 < 0xf4 < 0xf6, not by Value::cmp

Cross-type ordering is once-gated so a regression at lib.rs:556-560
that delegated to Value::cmp trips on every run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -454,5 +454,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_cbor_map_canonical_relations"
+path = "fuzz_targets/cbor_map_canonical_relations.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
